message:
feat: CLI — npx textsift {redact,detect,table,classify,download,cache}

Same engine as the lib, exposed as a CLI binary via package.json
"bin" field. `npx textsift` Just Works once the package is published;
no separate install or import path needed.

Subcommands map 1:1 to PrivacyFilter methods:
  redact [file]     → filter.redact()
  detect [file]     → filter.detect() (--jsonl for jq pipelines)
  table [file]      → filter.redactTable() (--mode redact|synth|drop_column)
  classify [file]   → filter.classifyColumns()
  download          → warm the cache (CI prep)
  cache info|clear  → manage the on-disk model cache

Loader flags pass through to the new options committed in de8e95f:
  --cache-dir / --model / --model-source / --offline / --no-prompt

Each also reads the corresponding TEXTSIFT_* env var so CI can set
them once.

Stdin / file / --in-place I/O. Minimal RFC-4180 CSV parser (handles
quoted fields + escaped quotes). Streaming download progress bar on
stderr during the 770 MB first-run fetch.

`--offline` semantics tightened: when set, native failure no longer
silently falls through to WASM (whose own model fetcher bypasses the
offline gate). Strict-no-network as advertised.

14 subprocess tests covering every documented surface (help, version,
unknown command, redact via stdin/file/in-place, --synth, detect
JSON + JSONL, classify CSV, table synth + drop_column, cache info,
cache without subcommand, --offline cold-cache failure).

files.patch:
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@ PII detection and redaction that runs [openai/privacy-filter](https://huggingfac
 
 ## What this is
 
-One npm package, two entry points so browsers never bundle native code:
+One npm package, two entry points + a CLI:
 
 ```sh
 npm install textsift
@@ -23,6 +23,16 @@ import { PrivacyFilter } from "textsift/browser";
 import { PrivacyFilter } from "textsift";
 ```
 
+```sh
+# Same engine as a CLI — no install, no browser, no clipboard dance
+echo "Hi Alice, alice@example.com" | npx textsift redact
+npx textsift table customers.csv --header --mode synth > clean.csv
+npx textsift detect log.txt --jsonl | jq 'select(.label == "private_email")'
+TEXTSIFT_OFFLINE=1 npx textsift redact file.txt   # CI: fail if not pre-cached
+npx textsift download                              # pre-warm in CI
+npx textsift cache info                            # show cache location + size
+```
+
 Bundlers (Vite/Webpack/esbuild/etc.) resolve `textsift/browser` and never touch the native entry. Node code resolves `textsift` and gets the platform-native binding via `optionalDependencies`.
 
 The model is OpenAI's; the value here is packaging:

@@ -11,6 +11,9 @@
     "./browser": "./dist/browser/index.js",
     "./package.json": "./package.json"
   },
+  "bin": {
+    "textsift": "./dist/cli.js"
+  },
   "files": [
     "dist/",
     "LICENSE",
@@ -38,7 +41,7 @@
   "scripts": {
     "build": "npm run build:browser && npm run build:native",
     "build:browser": "mkdir -p dist/browser && npm run build:zig && npm run build:opt && node scripts/inline-wasm.mjs && bun build src/browser/index.ts --outdir dist/browser --target browser --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun x tsc --emitDeclarationOnly",
-    "build:native": "bash scripts/build-native.sh && bun build src/index.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external",
+    "build:native": "bash scripts/build-native.sh && bun build src/index.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external && bun build src/cli.ts --outdir dist --target node --format esm --sourcemap=external",
     "build:zig": "npm run build:zig-st && npm run build:zig-mt",
     "build:zig-st": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --name textsift -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv textsift.wasm dist/browser/",
     "build:zig-mt": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd+atomics+bulk_memory -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --shared-memory --import-memory --initial-memory=4194304 --max-memory=2147483648 --export=__stack_pointer --name textsift-mt -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv textsift-mt.wasm dist/browser/",
